overall_summary: Deep technological focus on agent financial governance and identity standards is contrasted by significant community pessimism regarding token performance. Development is shifting toward secure autonomous agent-to-agent commerce through frameworks like Dreamline x402 and the MAXIA marketplace.
key_facts: ELISA token experienced a 95% decline from its high, leading to extensive price speculation and negative market sentiment on April 7.
The Dreamline x402 framework was designed to implement 'fail-closed' security for AI agent payments to prevent unauthorized spending.
A new ElizaOS agent was successfully deployed on Hatcher.host using a one-click process with Groq integration and live logs.
PR #6709 was opened to fix TOON action parameters and suppress continuation loops for asynchronous task actions.
The AgentID initiative was advanced to align ElizaOS with W3C standards for decentralized identifiers (DIDs) for secure agent personas.
The project implemented a security fix by pinning the axios dependency to version 1.7.8 to mitigate supply chain vulnerabilities.
An MCP server named eliza received an A security grade on Loaditout, passing all seven evaluated criteria.
GitHub Issue #6704 reported that 'elizaos create' fails on macOS due to Bun's postinstall scripts not running correctly.
The MAXIA plugin was proposed to allow agents to natively buy/sell AI services and rent GPUs via the Akash Network.
open_questions: Anyone else tried Hatcher.host for deploying ElizaOS agents?
Is the eliza-agent login failure on X (Twitter) version-specific to the plugin-twitter or ElizaOS core?
Would the Eliza core team be open to @sint/eliza-plugin as a community plugin for capability token enforcement?

new_issues_prs.title: Fix/toon action params
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Fixes critical bugs in character communication formats and prevents feedback loops in terminal actions.

new_issues_prs.title: Plugin: MAXIA AI Marketplace
new_issues_prs.author: majorelalexis-stack
new_issues_prs.significance: Proposes a unified marketplace for agent-to-agent commerce including GPU rentals and token swaps.

new_issues_prs.title: feat: add plugin-mnemopay — economic memory for AI agents
new_issues_prs.author: t49qnsx7qt-kpanks
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces a system for agents to remember payment outcomes and build financial reputation.

new_issues_prs.title: Plugin: SafeAgent — Token safety checks
new_issues_prs.author: CryptoGenesisSecurity
new_issues_prs.significance: Provides honeypot and rug pull detection to protect trading agents across 6 EVM chains.

overall_focus.claim: The project is prioritizing 'trust infrastructure', specifically secure financial guardrails and verifiable agent identities (AgentID).

discord_updates.summary: Dominated by ELISA token price performance concerns (95% drop) and market comparisons, alongside successful one-click agent deployments on Hatcher.host.
Technical troubleshooting regarding Twitter/X login failures and version compatibility for plugin-twitter.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users report that the one-click deployment process on Hatcher.host is smooth and effective for new ElizaOS agents.
Significant community dissatisfaction and accusations of price manipulation regarding the ELISA token's rapid decline.

strategic_insights.insight: The development of AgentID and financial spend-caps suggests a move toward a high-trust environment where agents can independently rent hardware and trade.
implications_or_questions: How will the 11 billion token supply impact the long-term utility of the ELISA token in this new commerce model?

market_analysis.observation: ELISA token market cap stabilized around 5.5 million USD despite heavy sell pressure and negative sentiment.
market_analysis.relevance: Reflects a critical stress test for community belief versus holders hedging or exiting.
